intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears dormant/barren.
compositional_truth_check.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears dormant/barren.
compositional_truth_check.p3_black_pot: Present: White rabbit anchor confirmed. Minimal vegetative presence.
compositional_truth_check.p4_black_pot: Present: Shared with P3/P2 area. Single green stem/sprout visible.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The expected specimens (String of Nickels, Mexican Mint) are not visually detectable in their respective pots. Only a single, isolated green sprout remains in the P3/P4 cluster.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.p2: Empty soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.p3: Contains white rabbit anchor; no foliage.
plant_audit.p4: Contains a single, thin, green stem/sprout showing signs of chlorosis or extreme stress.

biome_observations.debris: White granular material (likely crushed eggshells) present in the P3/P4 pot as an intervention.
biome_observations.surface_conditions: No moss or secondary growth; desk surface is clear.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single green sprout in the P3/P4 pot.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_current: The sprout remains static. No growth, no new leaves, no recovery. The rest of the pots remain devoid of visible plant life throughout the sequence.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The lack of foliage across the majority of the pots suggests a systemic failure of the biome. The single remaining sprout is insufficient to sustain the registry's expected diversity.

anomalies: Presence of white granular debris (eggshells) in P3/P4.
Total absence of P1 and P2 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a near-total collapse of the indoor desk biome. P1 and P2 are effectively barren. P3 and P4 share a single, struggling sprout. The white rabbit anchor remains the only constant. The environment appears to have been subjected to significant stress, leading to the loss of the primary specimens.
